finish_answer:
## Carlos J. Alfonso â Biography

**Carlos Juan Alfonso** (born March 27, 1955, in Havana, Cuba) is a Cuban-American architect, real estate executive, and civic leader. He is best known as the founding principal and CEO of Alfonso Architects and Alliant Partners LLC, and as the former chairman of the University of Florida Board of Trustees.

### Early Life
Alfonso was born in Havana, Cuba, to architect Carlos Ernesto Alfonso. In 1960, at the age of five, his family fled Cuba for the United States. He was raised in Tampa, Florida, and graduated from Jesuit High School in 1973. He earned both a Bachelor's of Design and a Master of Architecture from the University of Florida in 1981.

### Career â Alfonso Architects
In 1988, Carlos founded **Alfonso Architects, Inc.** alongside his father Carlos Ernesto, his brother Alberto Alfonso, and friend Angel del Monte. The firm is based in Ybor City, Florida, in a restored 1887 train depot, and employs approximately 45 people. The firm won the **Florida A.I.A. Firm of the Year Award** in 2003. Notable projects include the Streamsong Resort, Tampa International Airport Airside C, Nielsen Media Research Global Technology Center, the Frank and Carol Morsani Center, and the Chihuly Collection at the Morean Arts Center. Carlos serves as CEO, while his brother Alberto serves as president and lead designer.

### Career â Alliant Partners
In 2000, Alfonso founded **Alliant Partners, LLC**, a commercial real estate investment company offering services from property brokerage to project construction management. As CEO, he has developed properties for clients including CVS, Walgreens, Publix, The Home Depot, Starbucks, AT&T Mobility, and UF Health & Shands, among others.

### University of Florida Service
Alfonso was a **founding member** of the University of Florida Board of Trustees in 2001, appointed by Governor Jeb Bush after a restructuring of higher education governance. He served as **Vice Chair (2009â2011)** and then **Chairman (2011â2012)**. After Governor Rick Scott declined to reappoint him, Alfonso withdrew from the board in 2012. UF President J. Bernard Machen credited Alfonso as instrumental in the university's increased tuition flexibility, innovation progress, and research infrastructure growth. He later served on the UF Presidential Selection Committee and currently chairs the UF Advisory Board for UF Online.

### Public and Civic Service
Alfonso served on the **Florida Constitution Revision Commission (1997â1998)** and has been involved with numerous organizations including the Florida Council of 100 (director), the Florida Wildlife Foundation (vice chair), the Super Bowl XLIII Board of Directors, the Florida TaxWatch Board, and others.

### Awards
- 2025 United States Special Operations Command Medal for Outstanding Contributions to Special Operations
- 2014 AIA Florida Design Awards â Award of Excellence for Streamsong Resort Lodge and Clubhouse
- 2014 Golf, Inc. â Streamsong Resort, U.S. Golf Clubhouse of the Year (3rd Place)
- Distinguished Alumni â University of Florida College of Architecture

### Personal Life
Alfonso is a commercial multi-engine, instrument-rated pilot. He married Dorothy Alfonso in 1988, and they have three children: Ariana, Carlos, and Isabella. He is a practicing Roman Catholic and a registered Republican.

[Source: Wikipedia â "Carlos J. Alfonso"]
